```go
func (t *TextArea) SetOffset(row, column int) *TextArea
```

SetOffset sets the text's offset, that is, the number of rows and columns skipped during drawing at the top or on the left, respectively. If wrapping is enabled, the column offset is ignored. These values may get adjusted automatically to ensure that some text is always visible.

<a name="TextArea.SetPlaceholder"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetPlaceholder](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L786>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetPlaceholder(placeholder string) *TextArea
```

SetPlaceholder sets the text to be displayed when the text area is empty.

<a name="TextArea.SetPlaceholderStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetPlaceholderStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L893>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetPlaceholderStyle(style tcell.Style) *TextArea
```

SetPlaceholderStyle sets the style of the placeholder text.

<a name="TextArea.SetSelectedStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetSelectedStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L887>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetSelectedStyle(style tcell.Style) *TextArea
```

SetSelectedStyle sets the style of the selected text.

<a name="TextArea.SetSize"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetSize](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L818>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetSize(rows, columns int) *TextArea
```

SetSize sets the screen size of the input element of the text area. The input element is always located next to the label which is always located in the top left corner. If any of the values are 0 or larger than the available space, the available space will be used.

<a name="TextArea.SetText"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetText](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L390>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetText(text string, cursorAtTheEnd bool) *TextArea
```

SetText sets the text of the text area. All existing text is deleted and replaced with the new text. Any edits are discarded, no undos are available. This function is typically only used to initialize the text area with a text after it has been created. To clear the text area's text \(again, no undos\), provide an empty string.

If cursorAtTheEnd is false, the cursor is placed at the start of the text. If it is true, it is placed at the end of the text. For very long texts, placing the cursor at the end can be an expensive operation because the entire text needs to be parsed and laid out.

If you want to set text and preserve undo functionality, use [TextArea.Replace](<#TextArea.Replace>) instead.

<a name="TextArea.SetTextStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetTextStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L876>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetTextStyle(style tcell.Style) *TextArea
```

SetTextStyle sets the style of the text.

<a name="TextArea.SetWordWrap"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetWordWrap](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L777>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetWordWrap(wrapOnWords bool) *TextArea
```

SetWordWrap sets the flag that causes lines that are longer than the available width to be wrapped onto the next line at spaces or after punctuation marks \(according to [Unicode Standard Annex \\\#14](<https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr14/>)\). This flag is ignored if the flag set with [TextArea.SetWrap](<#TextArea.SetWrap>) is false. The text area's default is word\-wrapping.

<a name="TextArea.SetWrap"></a>

### func \(\*TextArea\) [SetWrap](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textarea.go#L762>)

```go
func (t *TextArea) SetWrap(wrap bool) *TextArea
```

SetWrap sets the flag that, if true, leads to lines that are longer than the available width being wrapped onto the next line. If false, any characters beyond the available width are not displayed.

<a name="TextView"></a>

## type [TextView](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L153-L258>)

TextView is a component to display read\-only text. While the text to be displayed can be changed or appended to, there is no functionality that allows the user to edit it. For that, [TextArea](<#TextArea>) should be used.

TextView implements the io.Writer interface so you can stream text to it, appending to the existing text. This does not trigger a redraw automatically but if a handler is installed via [TextView.SetChangedFunc](<#TextView.SetChangedFunc>), you can cause it to be redrawn. \(See [TextView.SetChangedFunc](<#TextView.SetChangedFunc>) for more details.\)

Tab characters advance the text to the next tab stop at every [TabSize](<#TabSize>) screen columns, but only if the text is left\-aligned. If the text is centered or right\-aligned, tab characters are simply replaced with [TabSize](<#TabSize>) spaces.

Word wrapping is enabled by default. Use [TextView.SetWrap](<#TextView.SetWrap>) and [TextView.SetWordWrap](<#TextView.SetWordWrap>) to change this.

### Navigation

If the text view is set to be scrollable \(which is the default\), text is kept in a buffer which may be larger than the screen and can be navigated with Vim\-like key binds:

- h, left arrow: Move left.
- l, right arrow: Move right.
- j, down arrow: Move down.
- k, up arrow: Move up.
- g, home: Move to the top.
- G, end: Move to the bottom.
- Ctrl\-F, page down: Move down by one page.
- Ctrl\-B, page up: Move up by one page.

If the text is not scrollable, any text above the top visible line is discarded. This can be useful when you want to continuously stream text to the text view and only keep the latest lines.

Use [Box.SetInputCapture](<#Box.SetInputCapture>) to override or modify keyboard input.

### Styles / Colors

If dynamic colors are enabled via [TextView.SetDynamicColors](<#TextView.SetDynamicColors>), text style can be changed dynamically by embedding color strings in square brackets. This works the same way as anywhere else. See the package documentation for more information.

### Regions and Highlights

If regions are enabled via [TextView.SetRegions](<#TextView.SetRegions>), you can define text regions within the text and assign region IDs to them. Text regions start with region tags. Region tags are square brackets that contain a region ID in double quotes, for example:

```
We define a ["rg"]region[""] here.
```

A text region ends with the next region tag. Tags with no region ID \(\[""\]\) don't start new regions. They can therefore be used to mark the end of a region. Region IDs must satisfy the following regular expression:

```
[a-zA-Z0-9_,;: \-\.]+
```

Regions can be highlighted by calling the [TextView.Highlight](<#TextView.Highlight>) function with one or more region IDs. This can be used to display search results, for example.

The [TextView.ScrollToHighlight](<#TextView.ScrollToHighlight>) function can be used to jump to the currently highlighted region once when the text view is drawn the next time.

### Large Texts

The text view can handle reasonably large texts. It will parse the text as needed. For optimal performance, it is best to access or display parts of the text very far down only if really needed. For example, call [TextView.ScrollToBeginning](<#TextView.ScrollToBeginning>) before adding the text to the text view, to avoid scrolling the text all the way to the bottom, forcing a full\-text parse.

For even larger texts or "infinite" streams of text such as log files, you should consider using [TextView.SetMaxLines](<#TextView.SetMaxLines>) to limit the number of lines in the text view buffer. Or disable the text view's scrollability altogether \(using [TextView.SetScrollable](<#TextView.SetScrollable>)\). This will cause the text view to discard lines moving out of the visible area at the top.

See https://github.com/rivo/tview/wiki/TextView for an example.

```go
type TextView struct {
    sync.Mutex
    *Box
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTextView"></a>

### func [NewTextView](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L261>)

```go
func NewTextView() *TextView
```

NewTextView returns a new [TextView](<#TextView>).

<a name="TextView.BatchWriter"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [BatchWriter](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L927>)

```go
func (t *TextView) BatchWriter() *batchWriter
```

BatchWriter returns a new writer that can be used to write into the buffer but without Locking/Unlocking the buffer on every write, as [TextView.Write](<#TextView.Write>) and [TextView.Clear](<#TextView.Clear>) do. The lock will be acquired once when BatchWriter is called, and will be released when the returned writer is closed. Example:

```
tv := tview.NewTextView()
w := tv.BatchWriter()
defer w.Close()
w.Clear()
fmt.Fprintln(w, "To sit in solemn silence")
fmt.Fprintln(w, "on a dull, dark, dock")
fmt.Println(tv.GetText(false))
```

Note that using the batch writer requires you to manage any issues that may arise from concurrency yourself. See package description for details on dealing with concurrency.

<a name="TextView.Clear"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [Clear](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L612>)

```go
func (t *TextView) Clear() *TextView
```

Clear removes all text from the buffer. This triggers the "changed" callback.

```go
func (t *TextView) Draw(screen tcell.Screen)
```

Draw draws this primitive onto the screen.

```go
func (t *TextView) Focus(delegate func(p Primitive))
```

```go
func (t *TextView) GetDisabled() bool
```

GetDisabled returns whether or not the item is disabled / read\-only.

<a name="TextView.GetFieldHeight"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetFieldHeight](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L313>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetFieldHeight() int
```

<a name="TextView.GetFieldWidth"></a>

```go
func (t *TextView) GetFieldWidth() int
```

GetFieldWidth returns this primitive's field width.

<a name="TextView.GetHighlights"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetHighlights](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L722>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetHighlights() (regionIDs []string)
```

GetHighlights returns the IDs of all currently highlighted regions.

<a name="TextView.GetLabel"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetLabel](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L286>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetLabel() string
```

GetLabel returns the text to be displayed before the text view.

<a name="TextView.GetOriginalLineCount"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetOriginalLineCount](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L457>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetOriginalLineCount() int
```

GetOriginalLineCount returns the number of lines in the original text buffer, without applying any wrapping. This is an expensive call as it needs to iterate over the entire text. Note that any text that has been discarded due to [TextView.SetMaxLines](<#TextView.SetMaxLines>) or [TextView.SetScrollable](<#TextView.SetScrollable>) will not be part of the count.

<a name="TextView.GetRegionText"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetRegionText](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L764>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetRegionText(regionID string) string
```

GetRegionText returns the text of the first region with the given ID. If dynamic colors are enabled, style tags are stripped from the text.

If the region does not exist or if regions are turned off, an empty string is returned.

This function can be expensive if the specified region is way beyond the visible area of the text view as the text needs to be parsed until the region can be found, or if the region does not contain any text.

<a name="TextView.GetRegions"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetRegions](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L823>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetRegions(startRow int, tail bool) []*Region
```

GetRegions returns the regions in this [TextView](<#TextView>). If tail is set to false, only regions from the startRow row to the end of the currently visible area are returned. If tail is set to true, the regions below the visible area of the view are also included. Note that the latter will require parsing the entire text and can therefore be expensive. Setting startRow to a value larger than the last visible row results in nil being returned. To obtain only visible regions, set startRow to the current row offset \(see [TextView.GetScrollOffset](<#TextView.GetScrollOffset>)\) and tail to false.

Regions are returned in the order of their appearance in the text. Do not modify the returned [Region](<#Region>) objects. Region positions change when the text view is resized or when the text changes. Such changes will render the returned slice invalid.

If this function is called before the text view was drawn for the first time, the return value is undefined.

<a name="TextView.GetScrollOffset"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetScrollOffset](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L607>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetScrollOffset() (row, column int)
```

GetScrollOffset returns the number of rows and columns that are skipped at the top left corner when the text view has been scrolled.

<a name="TextView.GetText"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetText](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L427>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetText(stripAllTags bool) string
```

GetText returns the current text of this text view. If "stripAllTags" is set to true, any region/style tags are stripped from the text. Note that any text that has been discarded due to [TextView.SetMaxLines](<#TextView.SetMaxLines>) or [TextView.SetScrollable](<#TextView.SetScrollable>) will not be part of the returned text.

<a name="TextView.GetWrappedLineCount"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [GetWrappedLineCount](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L485>)

```go
func (t *TextView) GetWrappedLineCount() int
```

GetWrappedLineCount returns the number of lines in the text view, taking wrapping into account \(if activated\). This is an even more expensive call than [TextView.GetOriginalLineCount](<#TextView.GetOriginalLineCount>) as it needs to parse the text until the end and calculate the line breaks. It will also allocate memory for each line. Note that any text that has been discarded due to [TextView.SetMaxLines](<#TextView.SetMaxLines>) or [TextView.SetScrollable](<#TextView.SetScrollable>) will not be part of the count. Calling this method before the text view was drawn for the first time will assume no wrapping.

<a name="TextView.Highlight"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [Highlight](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L648>)

```go
func (t *TextView) Highlight(regionIDs ...string) *TextView
```

Highlight specifies which regions should be highlighted. If highlight toggling is set to true \(see [TextView.SetToggleHighlights](<#TextView.SetToggleHighlights>)\), the highlight of the provided regions is toggled \(i.e. highlighted regions are un\-highlighted and vice versa\). If toggling is set to false, the provided regions are highlighted and all other regions will not be highlighted \(you may also provide nil to turn off all highlights\).

For more information on regions, see class description. Empty region strings or regions not contained in the text are ignored.

Text in highlighted regions will be drawn inverted, i.e. with their background and foreground colors swapped.

If toggling is set to false, clicking outside of any region will remove all highlights.

This function is expensive if a specified region is in a part of the text that has not yet been parsed, as is typically the case for lines below the visible area.

<a name="TextView.InputHandler"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [InputHandler](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L1426>)

```go
func (t *TextView) InputHandler() func(event *tcell.EventKey, setFocus func(p Primitive))
```

InputHandler returns the handler for this primitive.

### func \(\*TextView\) [MouseHandler](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L1490>)

```go
func (t *TextView) MouseHandler() func(action MouseAction, event *tcell.EventMouse, setFocus func(p Primitive)) (consumed bool, capture Primitive)
```

MouseHandler returns the mouse handler for this primitive.

<a name="TextView.ScrollTo"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [ScrollTo](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L571>)

```go
func (t *TextView) ScrollTo(row, column int) *TextView
```

ScrollTo scrolls to the specified row and column \(both starting with 0\).

<a name="TextView.ScrollToBeginning"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [ScrollToBeginning](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L583>)

```go
func (t *TextView) ScrollToBeginning() *TextView
```

ScrollToBeginning scrolls to the top left corner of the text if the text view is scrollable.

<a name="TextView.ScrollToEnd"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [ScrollToEnd](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L596>)

```go
func (t *TextView) ScrollToEnd() *TextView
```

ScrollToEnd scrolls to the bottom left corner of the text if the text view is scrollable. Adding new rows to the end of the text view will cause it to scroll with the new data.

<a name="TextView.ScrollToHighlight"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [ScrollToHighlight](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L746>)

```go
func (t *TextView) ScrollToHighlight() *TextView
```

ScrollToHighlight will cause the visible area to be scrolled so that the highlighted regions appear in the visible area of the text view. This repositioning happens the next time the text view is drawn. It happens only once so you will need to call this function repeatedly to always keep highlighted regions in view.

Nothing happens if there are no highlighted regions or if the text view is not scrollable.

<a name="TextView.SetBackgroundColor"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetBackgroundColor](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L392>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetBackgroundColor(color tcell.Color) *Box
```

SetBackgroundColor overrides its implementation in Box to set the background color of this primitive. For backwards compatibility reasons, it also sets the background color of the main text element.

<a name="TextView.SetChangedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetChangedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L528>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetChangedFunc(handler func()) *TextView
```

SetChangedFunc sets a handler function which is called when the text of the text view has changed. This is useful when text is written to this [io.Writer](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#Writer>) in a separate goroutine. Doing so does not automatically cause the screen to be refreshed so you may want to use the "changed" handler to redraw the screen.

Note that to avoid race conditions or deadlocks, there are a few rules you should follow:

- You can call [Application.Draw](<#Application.Draw>) from this handler.
- You can call \[TextView.HasFocus\] from this handler.
- During the execution of this handler, access to any other variables from this primitive or any other primitive must be queued using [Application.QueueUpdate](<#Application.QueueUpdate>).

See package description for details on dealing with concurrency.

<a name="TextView.SetDisabled"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetDisabled](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L318>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetDisabled(disabled bool) FormItem
```

SetDisabled sets whether or not the item is disabled / read\-only.

<a name="TextView.SetDoneFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetDoneFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L536>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetDoneFunc(handler func(key tcell.Key)) *TextView
```

SetDoneFunc sets a handler which is called when the user presses on the following keys: Escape, Enter, Tab, Backtab. The key is passed to the handler.

<a name="TextView.SetDynamicColors"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetDynamicColors](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L494>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetDynamicColors(dynamic bool) *TextView
```

SetDynamicColors sets the flag that allows the text color to be changed dynamically with style tags. See class description for details.

<a name="TextView.SetFinishedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetFinishedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L555>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetFinishedFunc(handler func(key tcell.Key)) FormItem
```

SetFinishedFunc sets a callback invoked when the user leaves this form item.

<a name="TextView.SetFormAttributes"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetFormAttributes](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L561>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetFormAttributes(labelWidth int, labelColor, bgColor, fieldTextColor, fieldBgColor tcell.Color) FormItem
```

SetFormAttributes sets attributes shared by all form items.

<a name="TextView.SetHighlightedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetHighlightedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L549>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetHighlightedFunc(handler func(added, removed, remaining []string)) *TextView
```

SetHighlightedFunc sets a handler which is called when the list of currently highlighted regions change. It receives a list of region IDs which were newly highlighted, those that are not highlighted anymore, and those that remain highlighted.

Note that because regions are only determined when drawing the text view, this function can only fire for regions that have existed when the text view was last drawn.

<a name="TextView.SetLabel"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetLabel](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L280>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetLabel(label string) *TextView
```

SetLabel sets the text to be displayed before the text view.

<a name="TextView.SetLabelWidth"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetLabelWidth](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L292>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetLabelWidth(width int) *TextView
```

SetLabelWidth sets the screen width of the label. A value of 0 will cause the primitive to use the width of the label string.

<a name="TextView.SetMaxLines"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetMaxLines](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L370>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetMaxLines(maxLines int) *TextView
```

SetMaxLines sets the maximum number of lines for this text view. Lines at the beginning of the text will be discarded when the text view is drawn, so as to remain below this value. Only lines above the first visible line are removed.

Broken\-over lines via word/character wrapping are counted individually.

Note that [TextView.GetText](<#TextView.GetText>) will return the shortened text.

A value of 0 \(the default\) will keep all lines in place.

<a name="TextView.SetRegions"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetRegions](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L504>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetRegions(regions bool) *TextView
```

SetRegions sets the flag that allows to define regions in the text. See class description for details.

<a name="TextView.SetScrollable"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetScrollable](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L330>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetScrollable(scrollable bool) *TextView
```

SetScrollable sets the flag that decides whether or not the text view is scrollable. If false, text that moves above the text view's top row will be permanently deleted.

<a name="TextView.SetSize"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetSize](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L301>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetSize(rows, columns int) *TextView
```

SetSize sets the screen size of the main text element of the text view. This element is always located next to the label which is always located in the top left corner. If any of the values are 0 or larger than the available space, the available space will be used.

<a name="TextView.SetText"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetText](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L411>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetText(text string) *TextView
```

SetText sets the text of this text view to the provided string. Previously contained text will be removed. As with writing to the text view io.Writer interface directly, this does not trigger an automatic redraw but it will trigger the "changed" callback if one is set.

<a name="TextView.SetTextAlign"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetTextAlign](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L377>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetTextAlign(align int) *TextView
```

SetTextAlign sets the text alignment within the text view. This must be either AlignLeft, AlignCenter, or AlignRight.

<a name="TextView.SetTextColor"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetTextColor](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L383>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetTextColor(color tcell.Color) *TextView
```

SetTextColor sets the initial color of the text.

<a name="TextView.SetTextStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetTextStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L401>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetTextStyle(style tcell.Style) *TextView
```

SetTextStyle sets the initial style of the text. This style's background color also determines the background color of the main text element.

<a name="TextView.SetToggleHighlights"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetToggleHighlights](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L733>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetToggleHighlights(toggle bool) *TextView
```

SetToggleHighlights sets a flag to determine how regions are highlighted. When set to true, the [TextView.Highlight](<#TextView.Highlight>) function \(or a mouse click\) will toggle the provided/selected regions. When set to false, [TextView.Highlight](<#TextView.Highlight>) \(or a mouse click\) will simply highlight the provided regions.

<a name="TextView.SetWordWrap"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetWordWrap](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L353>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetWordWrap(wrapOnWords bool) *TextView
```

SetWordWrap sets the flag that, if true and if the "wrap" flag is also true \(see [TextView.SetWrap](<#TextView.SetWrap>)\), wraps according to \[Unicode Standard Annex \#14\].

This flag is ignored if the "wrap" flag is false.

<a name="TextView.SetWrap"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [SetWrap](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L341>)

```go
func (t *TextView) SetWrap(wrap bool) *TextView
```

SetWrap sets the flag that, if true, leads to lines that are longer than the available width being wrapped onto the next line. If false, any characters beyond the available width are not displayed.

<a name="TextView.Write"></a>

### func \(\*TextView\) [Write](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/textview.go#L888>)

```go
func (t *TextView) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write lets us implement the io.Writer interface.

<a name="Theme"></a>

## type [Theme](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/styles.go#L6-L18>)

Theme defines the colors used when primitives are initialized.

```go
type Theme struct {
    PrimitiveBackgroundColor    tcell.Color // Main background color for primitives.
    ContrastBackgroundColor     tcell.Color // Background color for contrasting elements.
    MoreContrastBackgroundColor tcell.Color // Background color for even more contrasting elements.
    BorderColor                 tcell.Color // Box borders.
    TitleColor                  tcell.Color // Box titles.
    GraphicsColor               tcell.Color // Graphics.
    PrimaryTextColor            tcell.Color // Primary text.
    SecondaryTextColor          tcell.Color // Secondary text (e.g. labels).
    TertiaryTextColor           tcell.Color // Tertiary text (e.g. subtitles, notes).
    InverseTextColor            tcell.Color // Text on primary-colored backgrounds.
    ContrastSecondaryTextColor  tcell.Color // Secondary text on ContrastBackgroundColor-colored backgrounds.
}
```

<a name="TreeNode"></a>

## type [TreeNode](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L19-L56>)

TreeNode represents one node in a tree view.

```go
type TreeNode struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


<a name="TreeNode.RemoveChild"></a>

<a name="TreeNode.SetChildren"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetChildren](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L111>)


<a name="TreeNode.SetColor"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetColor](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L220>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetColor(color tcell.Color) *TreeNode
```

SetColor sets the node's text color. For compatibility reasons, this also sets the background color of the selected text style. For more control over styles, use [TreeNode.SetTextStyle](<#TreeNode.SetTextStyle>) and [TreeNode.SetSelectedTextStyle](<#TreeNode.SetSelectedTextStyle>).

<a name="TreeNode.SetExpanded"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetExpanded](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L165>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetExpanded(expanded bool) *TreeNode
```

<a name="TreeNode.SetIndent"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetIndent](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L252>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetIndent(indent int) *TreeNode
```

SetIndent sets an additional indentation for this node's text. A value of 0 keeps the text as far left as possible with a minimum of line graphics. Any value greater than that moves the text to the right.

<a name="TreeNode.SetReference"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetReference](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L100>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetReference(reference interface{}) *TreeNode
```

SetReference allows you to store a reference of any type in this node. This will allow you to establish a mapping between the TreeView hierarchy and your internal tree structure.

<a name="TreeNode.SetSelectable"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetSelectable](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L152>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetSelectable(selectable bool) *TreeNode
```

SetSelectable sets a flag indicating whether this node can be selected by the user.

<a name="TreeNode.SetSelectedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetSelectedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L159>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetSelectedFunc(handler func()) *TreeNode
```

SetSelectedFunc sets a function which is called when the user selects this node by hitting Enter when it is selected.

<a name="TreeNode.SetSelectedTextStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetSelectedTextStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L238>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetSelectedTextStyle(style tcell.Style) *TreeNode
```

SetSelectedTextStyle sets the text style for this node when it is selected.

<a name="TreeNode.SetText"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetText](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L206>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetText(text string) *TreeNode
```

SetText sets the node's text which is displayed.

<a name="TreeNode.SetTextStyle"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [SetTextStyle](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L227>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) SetTextStyle(style tcell.Style) *TreeNode
```

SetTextStyle sets the text style for this node.

<a name="TreeNode.Walk"></a>

### func \(\*TreeNode\) [Walk](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L75>)

```go
func (n *TreeNode) Walk(callback func(node, parent *TreeNode) bool) *TreeNode
```

Walk traverses this node's subtree in depth\-first, pre\-order \(NLR\) order and calls the provided callback function on each traversed node \(which includes this node\) with the traversed node and its parent node \(nil for this node\). The callback returns whether traversal should continue with the traversed node's child nodes \(true\) or not recurse any deeper \(false\).

<a name="TreeView"></a>

## type [TreeView](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L299-L355>)

TreeView displays tree structures. A tree consists of nodes \(TreeNode objects\) where each node has zero or more child nodes and exactly one parent node \(except for the root node which has no parent node\).

The SetRoot\(\) function is used to specify the root of the tree. Other nodes are added locally to the root node or any of its descendents. See the TreeNode documentation for details on node attributes. \(You can use SetReference\(\) to store a reference to nodes of your own tree structure.\)

Nodes can be selected by calling SetCurrentNode\(\). The user can navigate the selection or the tree by using the following keys:

- j, down arrow, right arrow: Move \(the selection\) down by one node.
- k, up arrow, left arrow: Move \(the selection\) up by one node.
- g, home: Move \(the selection\) to the top.
- G, end: Move \(the selection\) to the bottom.
- J: Move \(the selection\) up one level \(if that node is selectable\).
- K: Move \(the selection\) to the last node one level down \(if any\).
- Ctrl\-F, page down: Move \(the selection\) down by one page.
- Ctrl\-B, page up: Move \(the selection\) up by one page.

Selected nodes can trigger the "selected" callback when the user hits Enter.

The root node corresponds to level 0, its children correspond to level 1, their children to level 2, and so on. Per default, the first level that is displayed is 0, i.e. the root node. You can call SetTopLevel\(\) to hide levels.

If graphics are turned on \(see SetGraphics\(\)\), lines indicate the tree's hierarchy. Alternative \(or additionally\), you can set different prefixes using SetPrefixes\(\) for different levels, for example to display hierarchical bullet point lists.

See https://github.com/rivo/tview/wiki/TreeView for an example.

```go
type TreeView struct {
    *Box
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTreeView"></a>

### func [NewTreeView](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L358>)

```go
func NewTreeView() *TreeView
```

NewTreeView returns a new [TreeView](<#TreeView>).

<a name="TreeView.Draw"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [Draw](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L695>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) Draw(screen tcell.Screen)
```

Draw draws this primitive onto the screen.

<a name="TreeView.GetCurrentNode"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetCurrentNode](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L394>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetCurrentNode() *TreeNode
```

GetCurrentNode returns the currently selected node or nil of no node is currently selected.

<a name="TreeView.GetPath"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetPath](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L402>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetPath(node *TreeNode) []*TreeNode
```

GetPath returns all nodes located on the path from the root to the given node, including the root and the node itself. If there is no root node, nil is returned. If there are multiple paths to the node, a random one is chosen and returned.

<a name="TreeView.GetRoot"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetRoot](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L376>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetRoot() *TreeNode
```

GetRoot returns the root node of the tree. If no such node was previously set, nil is returned.

<a name="TreeView.GetRowCount"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetRowCount](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L510>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetRowCount() int
```

GetRowCount returns the number of "visible" nodes. This includes nodes which fall outside the tree view's box but notably does not include the children of collapsed nodes. Note that this value is only up to date after the tree view has been drawn.

<a name="TreeView.GetScrollOffset"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetScrollOffset](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L502>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetScrollOffset() int
```

GetScrollOffset returns the number of node rows that were skipped at the top of the tree view. Note that when the user navigates the tree view, this value is only updated after the tree view has been redrawn.

<a name="TreeView.GetSelectedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [GetSelectedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L488>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) GetSelectedFunc() func(node *TreeNode)
```

GetSelectedFunc returns the function set with [TreeView.SetSelectedFunc](<#TreeView.SetSelectedFunc>) or nil if no such function has been set.

<a name="TreeView.InputHandler"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [InputHandler](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L802>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) InputHandler() func(event *tcell.EventKey, setFocus func(p Primitive))
```

InputHandler returns the handler for this primitive.

<a name="TreeView.MouseHandler"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [MouseHandler](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L869>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) MouseHandler() func(action MouseAction, event *tcell.EventMouse, setFocus func(p Primitive)) (consumed bool, capture Primitive)
```

MouseHandler returns the mouse handler for this primitive.

<a name="TreeView.Move"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [Move](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L521>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) Move(offset int) *TreeView
```

Move moves the selection \(if a node is currently selected\) or scrolls the tree view \(if there is no selection\), by the given offset \(positive values to move/scroll down, negative values to move/scroll up\). For selection changes, the offset refers to the number selectable, visible nodes. For scrolling, the offset refers to the number of visible nodes.

If the offset is 0, nothing happens.

<a name="TreeView.SetAlign"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetAlign](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L454>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetAlign(align bool) *TreeView
```

SetAlign controls the horizontal alignment of the node texts. If set to true, all texts except that of top\-level nodes will be placed in the same column. If set to false, they will indent with the hierarchy.

<a name="TreeView.SetChangedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetChangedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L474>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetChangedFunc(handler func(node *TreeNode)) *TreeView
```

SetChangedFunc sets the function which is called when the currently selected node changes, for example when the user navigates to a new tree node.

<a name="TreeView.SetCurrentNode"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetCurrentNode](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L387>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetCurrentNode(node *TreeNode) *TreeView
```

SetCurrentNode sets the currently selected node. Provide nil to clear all selections. Selected nodes must be visible and selectable, or else the selection will be changed to the top\-most selectable and visible node.

This function does NOT trigger the "changed" callback because the actual node that will be selected is not known until the tree is drawn. Triggering the "changed" callback is thus deferred until the next call to [TreeView.Draw](<#TreeView.Draw>).

<a name="TreeView.SetDoneFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetDoneFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L494>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetDoneFunc(handler func(key tcell.Key)) *TreeView
```

SetDoneFunc sets a handler which is called whenever the user presses the Escape, Tab, or Backtab key.

<a name="TreeView.SetGraphics"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetGraphics](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L461>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetGraphics(showGraphics bool) *TreeView
```

SetGraphics sets a flag which determines whether or not line graphics are drawn to illustrate the tree's hierarchy.

<a name="TreeView.SetGraphicsColor"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetGraphicsColor](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L467>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetGraphicsColor(color tcell.Color) *TreeView
```

SetGraphicsColor sets the colors of the lines used to draw the tree structure.

<a name="TreeView.SetPrefixes"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetPrefixes](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L446>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetPrefixes(prefixes []string) *TreeView
```

SetPrefixes defines the strings drawn before the nodes' texts. This is a slice of strings where each element corresponds to a node's hierarchy level, i.e. 0 for the root, 1 for the root's children, and so on \(levels will cycle\).

For example, to display a hierarchical list with bullet points:

```
treeView.SetGraphics(false).
  SetPrefixes([]string{"* ", "- ", "x "})
```

Deeper levels will cycle through the prefixes.

<a name="TreeView.SetRoot"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetRoot](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L369>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetRoot(root *TreeNode) *TreeView
```

SetRoot sets the root node of the tree.

<a name="TreeView.SetSelectedFunc"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetSelectedFunc](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L481>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetSelectedFunc(handler func(node *TreeNode)) *TreeView
```

SetSelectedFunc sets the function which is called when the user selects a node by pressing Enter on the current selection.

<a name="TreeView.SetTopLevel"></a>

### func \(\*TreeView\) [SetTopLevel](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/treeview.go#L430>)

```go
func (t *TreeView) SetTopLevel(topLevel int) *TreeView
```

SetTopLevel sets the first tree level that is visible with 0 referring to the root, 1 to the root's child nodes, and so on. Nodes above the top level are not displayed.

# box


## Index

# presentation

```go
import "github.com/rivo/tview/demos/presentation"
```

A presentation of the tview package, implemented with tview.

### Navigation

The presentation will advance to the next slide when the primitive demonstrated in the current slide is left \(usually by hitting Enter or Escape\). Additionally, the following shortcuts can be used:

- Ctrl\-N: Jump to next slide
- Ctrl\-P: Jump to previous slide

<a name="Slide"></a>

## type [Slide](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/demos/presentation/main.go#L26>)

Slide is a function which returns the slide's main primitive and its title. It receives a "nextSlide" function which can be called to advance the presentation to the next slide.

```go
type Slide func(nextSlide func()) (title string, content tview.Primitive)
```

## Index

- [type MarkovChain](<#MarkovChain>)

<a name="MarkovChain"></a>

## type [MarkovChain](<https://github.com/rivo/tview/blob/master/demos/textview/chat/main.go#L21-L25>)

MarkovChain implements a simple order of one Markov chain for text generation.

```go
type MarkovChain struct {
    Words  []string      // All unique words.
    Starts []int         // Indexes of starting words in Words.
    Chain  map[int][]int // Maps word index to possible next word indexes in Words. Ordered by frequency (highest first).
}
```
